_meta.description: Per-candidate AIPAC / pro-Israel-lobby contribution totals used by apply-aipac-adjustments.py to compute a bonus or penalty applied to the candidate's RESOLUTE Citizen total. Driven by Adam's stated methodology: 'configure any candidate receiving AIPAC funding to be heavily penalized in their score in proportion to the millions they've gotten in campaign contributions; any who receive zero funds should be boosted by at least 7 points.'
_meta.rationale: AIPAC's super-PAC United Democracy Project routinely spends millions per cycle to influence U.S. congressional races on behalf of a foreign government's policy agenda. The scoring criterion in america_first/q3 already treats this as a failure cell; the adjustment layer translates the dollar-magnitude into a final-score impact so a casual reader sees the influence at a glance.
_meta.sources_required: Every entry in `entries` must cite at least one of: TrackAIPAC per-state page, OpenSecrets donor search for the candidate, or a primary FEC filing URL. The `dollars` figure should be a documented career total (or current-cycle if career not available).
_meta.review_cadence: Refresh quarterly or whenever a high-profile cycle closes. apply-aipac-adjustments.py is idempotent and only changes records with explicit entries here.

thomas-massie.note: Consistently votes against Israel aid bills and foreign-aid bills generally. Documented as one of the few House Republicans who refuses AIPAC-aligned campaign support.

rashida-tlaib.note: Outspoken critic of AIPAC; UDP has spent against her in primaries. Documented zero on the receiving side.

ilhan-omar.note: Outspoken critic of AIPAC; UDP has spent against her in primaries.

rand-paul.note: Long-standing foreign-aid skeptic; consistently opposes Israel aid bills and aid to other foreign governments.

marjorie-taylor-greene.note: Has shifted to publicly oppose Israel aid packages; documented zero on the receiving side.

chuck-schumer.note: Senate Democratic Leader; career AIPAC + pro-Israel-lobby contributions among the highest in Congress. Specific dollar figure approximate; refine from TrackAIPAC + OpenSecrets per quarterly review.

lindsey-graham.note: Vocal Israel advocate in the Senate; documented top-tier recipient.

ted-cruz.note: Vocal Israel advocate in the Senate; documented top-tier recipient.

cory-booker.note: AIPAC-aligned Democrat; UDP has supported him.

tim-scott.note: Vocal Israel advocate in the Senate; documented top-tier recipient.

john-fetterman.note: Strongly pro-Israel Democrat since taking office; AIPAC-aligned.

rick-scott.note: Reliable Senate vote on Israel-related bills; documented mid-tier recipient.

ashley-moody.note: FL Senator (sworn in early 2025); shorter Senate-finance window than peers but documented AIPAC-aligned.

james-fishback.note: FL Gov 2026 R primary candidate. Public-position record includes support for state divestment from Israel Bonds — a stance opposite of most R candidates who accept AIPAC-aligned support. Documented zero on the receiving side as of his current run.
